summary.title: [Screenshots] Sources blur on high-density displays
summary.one_line_description: The responsive screenshot pipeline offers only one low-density candidate per viewport, causing app text and controls to upscale and lose sharpness.

bug_1.file_locations: - demo/src/components/elements/responsive-screenshot.tsx – function: `VerifiedAppScreenshot`, Lines 42-75 - demo/src/components/elements/responsive-screenshot.tsx – functions: `HeroScreenshot`, `FeatureScreenshotLeft`, and `FeatureScreenshotRight`, Lines 81-124 - demo/public/img/screenshots/bulma-policy-advisor-workspace-mobile.webp – 390×330 mobile capture - demo/public/img/screenshots/bulma-policy-advisor-workspace.webp – 1440×900 desktop capture - demo/public/img/screenshots/bulma-policy-evidence-ledger-mobile.webp – 390×350 mobile capture - demo/public/img/screenshots/bulma-policy-evidence-ledger.webp – 710×510 desktop capture - demo/public/img/screenshots/bulma-lender-comparison-mobile.webp – 350×305 mobile capture - demo/public/img/screenshots/bulma-lender-comparison.webp – 840×603 desktop capture

issue_description.description: `VerifiedAppScreenshot` gave each viewport exactly one unlabelled `srcSet` candidate, so the browser had no higher-density image to select for Retina and other high-DPI screens. The problem was visible even at 1× near the mobile breakpoint: browser verification at 639 CSS pixels rendered the 390-pixel hero at 472.81 pixels and rendered the 390-pixel evidence and 350-pixel comparison captures at 517.5 pixels. At the required 390-pixel viewport, the three captures rendered at 273.63-293.41 CSS pixels, requiring roughly 547-587 source pixels for a 2× display but receiving only 350-390 pixels. Desktop had the same limitation: at 1440×900 the 1440-pixel hero rendered 1072 CSS pixels, requiring 2144 source pixels at 2×. Because these captures contain small UI labels and table text, interpolation softened the exact content the visuals were intended to showcase.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Mobile selection** | A source with enough pixels for its rendered width and device density | At 639 CSS pixels, 350-390-pixel assets rendered at 472.81-517.5 pixels even at 1× | | **High-DPI rendering** | The browser selects a 2× candidate so small app text remains crisp | Only one candidate existed, so 2× screens upscaled the 1× asset | | **Desktop hero** | The 1072 CSS-pixel hero has at least 2144 source pixels on a 2× display | The only desktop hero source was 1440 pixels wide | | **Visual result** | Policy labels, evidence dates, and lender-table text retain capture sharpness | Browser interpolation softened the UI details on common high-density devices |

summary.title: [Structured Data] Homepage publishes synthetic screenshot
summary.one_line_description: The homepage Organization JSON-LD still advertises the retired synthetic app mock instead of one of the verified Policy Advisor captures.

bug_2.file_locations: - demo/src/schemas/organization-schema.ts – `organizationSchema.image`, Line 22 - demo/src/app/page.tsx – `homeStructuredData` and homepage JSON-LD output, Lines 163-183

issue_description.description: The three visible homepage placements loaded distinct verified Policy Advisor assets, but `organizationSchema.image` still pointed to `/img/screenshots/1.webp`, the retired synthetic mock that the change replaced. `Page` includes that schema in `homeStructuredData` and serialises it into the rendered `script#structured-data`, so every homepage response continued to publish the stale visual to search-engine consumers even though the DOM images used the new `bulma-*` files. This left an externally consumed homepage image reference inconsistent with the visible product presentation and violated the requirement that no stale synthetic source references remain.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Homepage image references** | Visible images and Organization JSON-LD reference approved current Policy Advisor captures | Visible images used `bulma-*` assets, but Organization JSON-LD referenced `1.webp` | | **External representation** | Search consumers receive a current, accurate Bulma application visual | Search consumers were directed to the retired synthetic mock | | **Old-reference removal** | No runtime homepage output contains the stale synthetic screenshot path | `script#structured-data` contained `https://bulma.com.au/img/screenshots/1.webp` |
